codeReview.summary: Reviewed the original trimming-loop implementation and the follow-up reversal fix. The fix updates closed-loop reversal to reverse edge order and per-edge direction together, then re-closes the UV polyline, which directly addresses the prior malformed-loop failure in `classify_loops`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `rust-bop-worker` skill still recommends `cargo test -p truck-bop <test_name> -- --exact` in the RED step without warning that mission feature IDs often do not match Rust test names, which led the original worker to a misleading 0-test success run.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Original handoff records `cargo test -p truck-bop trimming_loops -- --exact` returning 0 tests; `.factory/skills/rust-bop-worker/SKILL.md` still presents the exact-name command without that caveat.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared library documents other face-splitting invariants, but it still does not capture the trimming-loop reversal rule that closed loops must reverse edge order and per-edge UV direction together, then be re-closed so `first == last` remains true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/library/architecture.md` notes face splitting only at a high level, and `.factory/library/truck-bop.md` has no trimming-loop note, while `truck-bop/src/trim.rs:179` and `truck-bop/src/trim.rs:201` now encode this specific invariant.

summary: Re-reviewed `trimming-loops` together with fix feature `fix-trimming-loop-reversal`. I checked the prior failed review, both handoffs, the fix transcript skeleton, and both diffs (`1c6c5c8e2986c02b3908f77d5b41c7d386cadc86` and `3b6ab1901836aef05226469b771dc2f607a1c6e8`). The fix adequately resolves the original blocking issue: closed-loop reversal now preserves closure and edge connectivity, and no remaining blocking issues were identified in scope.
